name: Min Soo Kim
tagline: Building safer, more grounded language and multimodal models.
bio: I am a graduate student at Yonsei University, advised by Prof. Albert No in AI-ISL. I work on `natural language processing`, `multimodal learning`, and `AI safety`. My research focuses on understanding where large language and vision-language models fail — from hallucination and jailbreaks to distribution shift — and on designing evaluation protocols that remain reliable under adversarial use. More broadly, I am interested in building AI systems that are not only capable, but also robust, interpretable, and trustworthy in real-world settings.
currently: Studying alignment of multimodal LLMs under distribution shift
